questions.q: この英文の内容として最も適切なものを1つ選びなさい。
questions.a: トニーは毎晩、レストランで夕食を終えて帰ろうとする客を呼び止めてマジックを見せていた。
questions.b: 私はプロのマジシャンで、ニューヨークのレストランで友人のトニーにマジックを披露していた。
questions.c: プロのマジシャンであるトニーは、客が食事をしている間、毎晩ニューヨークのレストランでマジックを見せていた。
questions.d: トニーはレストランの客の一人として、毎晩ほかのマジシャンのマジックを楽しんでいた。
questions.explanation: 本文は「客が夕食をとる間(as they ate their dinners)」に毎晩マジックを見せたと述べるので、食事中を正しく言い換えたCが正解。Aは「夕食を終えて」帰る客とする点で時間関係が逆。Bはマジシャンを「私」とする主客の取り違えで、実際に演じたのはトニー。Dはトニーを演者ではなく客とする役割の取り違えで本文と矛盾する。

questions.q: Which one has the closest meaning to the sentence above?
questions.a: Tony worked at a New York restaurant, where every evening he watched other magicians perform tricks for the guests as they had dinner.
questions.b: Tony, a friend of mine and a professional magician, performed tricks every night at a New York restaurant while the customers were eating their meals.
questions.c: Every evening at a New York restaurant, the guests performed magic tricks for Tony while they were eating their dinners.
questions.d: Tony, a professional magician, performed at a New York restaurant only after the guests had completely finished their dinners.
questions.explanation: The original says Tony himself performed magic each evening while the guests were eating, so B, which rewords this with "performed tricks ... while the customers were eating," matches. A is wrong because it makes Tony a watcher of other magicians, not the performer. C reverses who performs (the guests, not Tony), and D changes the timing to "after ... finished," while the original means during the meal.
